Created page with "{{Citation required|date=July 2022}} '''Yoshuku Performing Arts''' (予祝芸能) refers to [[traditional performing arts]] viewed from the perspective of [[faith]], including celebratory [[performing arts]] such as [[Manzai]] and [[Harukoma]], which commemorate the New Year, as well as [[Ta-asobi]] and [[Onda]] rituals, where agricultural activities are enacted in advance to pray for a successful rice harvest. The practice involves preemptively celebrating to pray for..."

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:予祝芸能]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11371101]]
Wikidata name: yoshuku geinō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Citation required|date=July 2022}}
'''Yoshuku Performing Arts''' (予祝芸能) refers to forms of {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=traditional performing arts|ja|伝統芸能|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theatre of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の伝統芸能|1=theatre of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1364447}} viewed from a perspective of [[:en:Faith|faith]], such as {{ill|Manzai|ja|萬歳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manzai|qq=|1=manzai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620484}} and [[:en:Hobby horse (toy)|Harukoma]], which celebrate the New Year, or performances like {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ota|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}} that simulate agricultural work to pray for a bountiful rice harvest.

These arts embody the act of preemptive celebration to pray for the realization of blessings, rooted in the belief and thought process associated with [[:en:Kotodama|Kotodama]]. Similar characteristics can be observed in the "Yashiki-bome" songs of [[:en:Lion dance|lion dances]] or {{ill|furyu dances|ja|風流踊り|1=Furyū-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dances|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 風流踊}}. In contrast to Yoshuku Performing Arts, forms of art with a stronger focus on warding off disasters are sometimes referred to as {{ill|Josai Performing Arts|ja|除災芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Josai Performing Arts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Yoshuku|ja|予祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshuku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kadotsuke|ja|門付|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadotsuke|qq=}}

== External Links ==
* {{kotobank|Yoshuku}}
* {{kotobank|Yoshuku Events}}
* {{kotobank|Yoshuku Rituals}}
* {{kotobank|Hokai}}
* {{kotobank|Toshihi・Iwahi・Gahi}}
* {{kotobank|Iwahi}}

Wikidata name: yoshuku geinō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Citation required|date=July 2022}}
'''Yoshuku Performing Arts''' (予祝芸能) refers to forms of {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=traditional performing arts|ja|伝統芸能|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theatre of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の伝統芸能|1=theatre of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1364447}} viewed from a perspective of [[:en:Faith|faith]], such as {{ill|Manzai|ja|萬歳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manzai|1=manzai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620484}} and [[:en:Hobby horse (toy)|Harukoma]], which celebrate the New Year, or performances like {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ota|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}} that simulate agricultural work to pray for a bountiful rice harvest.

These arts embody the act of preemptive celebration to pray for the realization of blessings, rooted in the belief and thought process associated with [[:en:Kotodama|Kotodama]]. Similar characteristics can be observed in the "Yashiki-bome" songs of [[:en:Lion dance|lion dances]] or {{ill|furyu dances|ja|風流踊り|1=Furyū-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dances|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 風流踊}}. In contrast to Yoshuku Performing Arts, forms of art with a stronger focus on warding off disasters are sometimes referred to as {{ill|Josai Performing Arts|ja|除災芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Josai Performing Arts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

{{Citation required|date=July 2022}}
'''Yoshuku Performing Arts''' (予祝芸能) refers to forms of {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=traditional performing arts|ja|伝統芸能|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theatre of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の伝統芸能|1=theatre of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1364447}} viewed from a perspective of [[:en:Faith|faith]], such as {{ill|Manzai|ja|萬歳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manzai|1=manzai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620484}} and [[:en:Hobby horse (toy)|Harukoma]], which celebrate the New Year, or performances like {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ota|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}} that simulate agricultural work to pray for a bountiful rice harvest.

These arts embody the act of preemptive celebration to pray for the realization of blessings, rooted in the belief and thought process associated with [[:en:Kotodama|Kotodama]]. Similar characteristics can be observed in the "Yashiki-bome" songs of [[:en:Lion dance|lion dances]] or {{ill|furyu dances|ja|風流踊り|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dances|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 風流踊}}. In contrast to Yoshuku Performing Arts, forms of art with a stronger focus on warding off disasters are sometimes referred to as {{ill|Josai Performing Arts|ja|除災芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Josai Performing Arts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Yoshuku|ja|予祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshuku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kadotsuke|ja|門付|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadotsuke|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:予祝芸能]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11371101]]
Wikidata name: yoshuku geinō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Citation required|date=July 2022}}
'''Yoshuku Performing Arts''' (予祝芸能) refers to forms of {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=traditional performing arts|ja|伝統芸能|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theatre of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の伝統芸能|1=theatre of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1364447}} viewed from a perspective of [[:en:Faith|faith]], such as {{ill|Manzai|ja|萬歳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manzai|1=manzai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620484}} and [[:en:Hobby horse (toy)|Harukoma]], which celebrate the New Year, or performances like {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ota|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}} that simulate agricultural work to pray for a bountiful rice harvest.

These arts embody the act of preemptive celebration to pray for the realization of blessings, rooted in the belief and thought process associated with [[:en:Kotodama|Kotodama]]. Similar characteristics can be observed in the "Yashiki-bome" songs of [[:en:Lion dance|lion dances]] or {{ill|furyu dances|ja|風流踊り|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dances|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 風流踊}}. In contrast to Yoshuku Performing Arts, forms of art with a stronger focus on warding off disasters are sometimes referred to as {{ill|Josai Performing Arts|ja|除災芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Josai Performing Arts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Yoshuku|ja|予祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshuku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kadotsuke|ja|門付|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadotsuke|qq=}}

Similar characteristics can be observed in the "Yashiki-bome" songs of [[:en:Lion dance|lion dances]] or {{ill|furyu dances|ja|風流踊り|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dances|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 風流踊}}. In contrast to Yoshuku Performing Arts, forms of art with a stronger focus on warding off disasters are sometimes referred to as {{ill|Josai Performing Arts|ja|除災芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Josai Performing Arts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Yoshuku|ja|予祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshuku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kadotsuke|ja|門付|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadotsuke|qq=}}
